problem.title: CT-ICP performance-priority trade-off on the public ROS1 HDL-400 window with synthesized per-point time
problem.question: Which CT-ICP odometry profile should stay as the current default when ATE matters more than throughput on the public ROS1 HDL-400 bag reconstructed with scan-order synthetic point timestamps?

dataset.reference_role: public reference trajectory converted from pose_trace.csv
dataset.timestamp_note: The public ROS1 bag lacks a native time field, so per-point time is synthesized from scan order.

variants.label: Balanced window
variants.intent: Keep the current repository default as the continuous-time baseline.

variants.label: Fast window
variants.intent: Shrink the working set and point budget to improve FPS.

variants.label: Dense window
variants.intent: Increase point density and window richness to preserve more structure when drift matters more than throughput.
